title: Boat Rental in Samoa: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
Samoa offers some of the South Pacific's most affordable boating, with half-day rentals from Apia Harbour starting at around €250. Whether you want to explore the coral gardens of the Aleipata Islands or snorkel in the To Sua Ocean Trench, BoatHire24 connects you with local operators who offer genuine value.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Samoa?

Expect to pay between €250 and €1,500 per day depending on the vessel and season. Shared tours start around €80 per person for a half-day trip. Private motorboats can be hired from €400 for four hours, while a crewed catamaran for a full day typically ranges from €900 to €1,500. For the best deals, compare day charters in Samoa during the dry season when demand is lower.

Where to set off in Samoa

Most boating activity centres on Upolu and Savai'i. The main departure points include Apia Harbour on Upolu, the Salelologa Wharf on Savai'i, and the marina at the Aggie Grey's Lagoon Resort. Other popular spots are Mulifanua Harbour (for trips to Manono Island) and the sheltered bay of Lalomanu, which provides easy access to the outer reef. These locations offer basic facilities like fuel and moorings, though advance booking is recommended.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (25-28ft), ideal for couples or small groups exploring the coastline; sleeps 4-6; from €250 half-day.
  2. Speedboat / RIB (28-32ft), perfect for reaching the Aleipata Islands or diving spots; capacity 8-10; from €400 half-day.
  3. Sailing catamaran (38-45ft), stable and spacious for day trips with families; up to 12 guests; from €800 full-day.
  4. Crewed luxury yacht (50-60ft), includes skipper and crew for a premium experience; up to 10 guests; from €1,200 full-day. For example, the Limpopo charter page features an Axopar 28ft boat that is well-suited for reef tours.

Cost breakdown for Samoa boat hire

  • Shared snorkelling tour (4 hours): €80-€120 per person, includes guide and equipment.
  • Private half-day motorboat (4 hours, 8 guests): €350-€500, fuel included.
  • Full-day catamaran (8 hours, 12 guests): €900-€1,200, with soft drinks and snacks.
  • Crewed yacht with overnight option (24 hours, 10 guests): €1,200-€1,500, including crew and lunch.

When to go and do you need a licence?

The best season for boating is May to October when the southeast trade winds make for calm seas. During these months, the weather is drier and visibility is excellent. The local wind, known as the 'Ala'ala, can pick up in June, but generally conditions remain pleasant. Most boat rentals do not require a licence if you hire a skipper; bareboat charter may require a valid ICC or equivalent. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking.

If you plan to visit during the wet season (November to April), be aware that afternoon squalls are common. Always carry a waterproof bag for electronics and book a boat with a canopy.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80-€1,500 per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August (high demand)
  • Trip length: 4 hours to 7 days
  • Typical capacity: 4-12 guests
  • Currency: EUR (quoted by operators)

Questions about boat rental in Samoa

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Samoa?

Yes, most operators offer skippered charters where a captain is included, so no licence is needed.

How far is it from Apia to the Aleipata Islands by boat?

The distance is roughly 15 nautical miles, taking about 45 minutes in a fast motorboat.

What type of boat is best for snorkelling in Samoa?

A small powerboat or RIB is ideal as it can get close to the reef and has space for gear.

Are there any hidden costs for boat hire in 2026?

Some operators charge a separate fuel surcharge or park entry fees, so always check the total price before booking.

Whether you are a first-time renter or an experienced boater, finding a suitable vessel is straightforward on the BoatHire24 marketplace. If you own a boat in Samoa, listing it with us costs nothing upfront and connects you with travellers from around the world.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Samoa and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
